metadata.description: Intent classification dataset for Bart chatbot

messages.text: I need help resetting my password.
messages.notes: Clear problem statement, no ambiguity

messages.text: My account got locked out and I can't get back in.
messages.notes: Problem framing with temporal context

messages.text: I've been trying to update my billing info for the past hour and it keeps failing.
messages.notes: Frustration signal + repeated attempt = high confidence direct request

messages.text: How do I export my data?
messages.notes: Simple how-to question, clear intent

messages.text: Can someone walk me through the refund process?
messages.notes: Addressed to 'someone' broadly, but still a clear request

messages.text: I got charged twice for the same invoice.
messages.notes: Factual complaint, implies resolution is needed

messages.text: Where do I find my API key?
messages.notes: Navigation/how-to question, clear intent

messages.text: I need to add a new user to my team but I don't see that option anywhere.
messages.notes: Feature discovery + implied frustration

messages.text: Can I downgrade my plan without losing my data?
messages.notes: Pre-action clarification question

messages.text: The app crashes every time I try to open a report.
messages.notes: Bug report with reproducible trigger

messages.text: I need to update the email address on my account.
messages.notes: Account management task, unambiguous

messages.text: What's included in the Pro plan?
messages.notes: Informational request about product

messages.text: I'm getting a 403 error when I try to access the admin panel.
messages.notes: Technical error report, specific and actionable

messages.text: How long does it take to process a cancellation?
messages.notes: Process timeline question

messages.text: I submitted a support ticket three days ago and haven't gotten a response.
messages.notes: Escalation signal, follow-up on unresolved issue

messages.text: Is there a way to bulk import contacts?
messages.notes: Feature inquiry, product knowledge request

messages.text: My two-factor authentication stopped working after I got a new phone.
messages.notes: Device change causing auth issue, common support scenario

messages.text: I want to transfer ownership of my account to someone else.
messages.notes: Account ownership change request

messages.text: Hey Bart, can you help me cancel my subscription?
messages.notes: Explicitly names bot, clear request

messages.text: Bart, what are the current plan options?
messages.notes: Explicit bot name, informational request

messages.text: @Bart I submitted a ticket yesterday but haven't heard back.
messages.notes: @ mention style, follow-up on prior action

messages.text: Bart can you look into why my invoice is wrong?
messages.notes: Explicit bot name, investigative request

messages.text: Hey Bart — is there a way to disable email notifications?
messages.notes: Casual address with feature question

messages.text: Bart, I think I was overcharged this month.
messages.notes: Billing dispute, bot named directly

messages.text: Quick question for Bart: do you support SSO?
messages.notes: Third-person reference to bot within the message

messages.text: @Bart what's the best way to reach a human agent?
messages.notes: Bot asked to route to human, escalation intent

messages.text: Bart, my teammate says they never got the invite I sent.
messages.notes: Relay of third-party complaint, explicit bot address

messages.text: Hey Bart, does the free trial require a credit card?
messages.notes: Pre-signup question, sales-adjacent

messages.text: Bart — I need to pause my account for a couple months, is that possible?
messages.notes: Unusual account action, bot addressed with em dash

messages.text: Hi Bart, can you confirm my cancellation went through?
messages.notes: Confirmation request, polite greeting before bot name

messages.text: Bart are you able to pull up order #8823?
messages.notes: Specific record lookup, capability-checking framing

messages.text: Bart, what happens if I miss a payment?
messages.notes: Policy question, explicit bot address

messages.text: Can you help me with something?
messages.notes: Could be directed at bot or another user in chat

messages.text: Sorry, I was talking to Bart.
messages.notes: Retroactively confirms turn 1 was intended for the bot

messages.text: Is there any way to get a refund on that charge?
messages.notes: Sounds like it could be directed at a human agent

messages.text: Oh wait, I was asking Bart, not you.
messages.notes: Redirect confirms original message was for bot

messages.text: Do you know when the outage will be resolved?
messages.notes: 'You' is ambiguous in group chat context

messages.text: That was for Bart by the way.
messages.notes: Clarification turns ambiguous message into bot-directed request

messages.text: Can you pull up my account details?
messages.notes: Reasonable to assume a human agent might be addressed

messages.text: Not you Mike, I was talking to Bart.
messages.notes: Names both the human and the bot, fully disambiguates

messages.text: What's the status on ticket #4821?
messages.notes: Could be directed at a human support agent

messages.text: Whoops, meant to ask Bart that.
messages.notes: Self-correcting redirect

messages.text: Do you have a record of my last payment?
messages.notes: Could be asking a human or the bot

messages.text: I was talking to Bart, sorry for the confusion.
messages.notes: Polite disambiguation, confirms bot intent

messages.text: Can you check if my upgrade went through?
messages.notes: Verification request, addressee unclear

messages.text: Bart, that question was for you.
messages.notes: Direct address in follow-up message

messages.text: How long does onboarding usually take?
messages.notes: Could be asking a team member or the bot

messages.text: lol sorry that was for Bart not you
messages.notes: Informal tone, still a valid clarification

messages.text: Is it possible to get an itemized receipt?
messages.notes: Billing question, no clear addressee

messages.text: Hey Bart — that one was directed at you.
messages.notes: Explicit redirect with casual tone

messages.text: What integrations do you support?
messages.notes: 'You' could mean the company, a person, or the bot

messages.text: I was asking Bart, not the team.
messages.notes: Distinguishes bot from team, confirms bot intent

messages.text: Can you reopen that ticket for me?
messages.notes: Action request with no clear addressee

messages.text: Talking to Bart on that one.
messages.notes: Short, informal clarification

messages.text: Are there any known issues right now?
messages.notes: Status question, often asked of support team or bot

messages.text: Bart — that was meant for you, not the group.
messages.notes: Distinguishes bot from group channel, explicit

messages.text: I think there's a bug in the reporting module.
messages.notes: Observation that implies a report, no explicit ask or addressee

messages.text: FYI Bart, that was aimed at you.
messages.notes: FYI framing with explicit bot address

messages.text: My discount code isn't working at checkout.
messages.notes: Problem report without a named recipient

messages.text: Just to clarify, I was talking to Bart.
messages.notes: Explicit clarification with 'just to clarify' hedge

messages.text: Is there a mobile app available?
messages.notes: Product availability question, could be asked of anyone

messages.text: Directed at Bart, not asking the room.
messages.notes: Clear disambiguation between bot and room

messages.text: How do I switch between workspaces?
messages.notes: How-to question, no explicit addressee

messages.text: Oops — Bart, that was for you.
messages.notes: Self-correction with casual tone

messages.text: Hey can you check on that for me?
messages.notes: Vague, no clear addressee

messages.text: I wasn't talking to Bart, I meant Sarah.
messages.notes: Confirms message was NOT for the bot — bot should not respond

messages.text: Can you send me that file again?
messages.notes: File requests are typically between humans

messages.text: That wasn't for Bart lol, talking to Jake.
messages.notes: Casual correction, bot should stand down

messages.text: Did you get a chance to review the doc I shared?
messages.notes: Document review request, likely human-to-human

messages.text: Not Bart — meant for the team.
messages.notes: Bot explicitly excluded, message is for the team

messages.text: Are you free for a call later?
messages.notes: Calendar/availability question, almost certainly human-targeted

messages.text: Haha not Bart, asking you Dave.
messages.notes: Humor signals this is obviously not bot-directed, names human

messages.text: What did legal say about the contract?
messages.notes: Internal business question, unlikely to be bot-directed

messages.text: That's for Anna, not Bart.
messages.notes: Explicit correction naming the intended recipient

messages.text: Can you cover for me tomorrow?
messages.notes: Shift coverage request, definitively human-targeted

messages.text: Wasn't asking Bart obviously, this is for Tom.
messages.notes: 'Obviously' signals the speaker knows it's off-target for a bot

messages.text: Do you have the login for that staging environment?
messages.notes: Credential sharing request, human-to-human

messages.text: Directed at Marcus, not Bart.
messages.notes: Clean explicit correction

messages.text: Hey, are you joining the 3pm sync?
messages.notes: Meeting attendance question, clearly human-targeted

messages.text: That was for Priya, not the bot.
messages.notes: Refers to Bart as 'the bot', explicit correction

messages.text: Can you double check those numbers before we send it out?
messages.notes: Pre-send review request, likely peer-to-peer

messages.text: Sorry — not for Bart, asking Chris.
messages.notes: Apologetic correction, names both non-recipient and recipient

messages.text: Did anyone see my message earlier?
messages.notes: Meta-message about a prior message, group-directed

messages.text: Not asking Bart — just calling out to the channel.
messages.notes: Clarifies it was a broadcast, not a bot query

messages.text: Can you approve this expense report?
messages.notes: Approval workflow request, requires a human decision-maker

messages.text: That's for my manager, definitely not Bart.
messages.notes: Emphatic correction — 'definitely not Bart'

messages.text: Let me know when you've pushed the changes.
messages.notes: Dev workflow coordination, peer-targeted

messages.text: For the devs, not Bart.
messages.notes: Group label ('the devs') instead of individual name

messages.text: I've been a customer for three years and this is really frustrating.
messages.notes: Venting with implied request for help, no explicit ask

messages.text: Something seems off with my dashboard since the update.
messages.notes: Observation framing, implicitly asking for investigation

messages.text: I wonder if there's a faster way to do this.
messages.notes: 'I wonder' framing softens a request for guidance

messages.text: Not sure if this is the right channel but my team can't access the shared workspace.
messages.notes: Hedge + problem statement = indirect but actionable

messages.text: It would be nice if there was a way to undo that last action.
messages.notes: Wishes for a feature, implicitly asking if it exists

messages.text: This error message doesn't tell me anything useful.
messages.notes: Complaint about UX, implicitly asking for help interpreting it

messages.text: I've tried everything and nothing is working.
messages.notes: Exhaustion signal, strong implicit request for assistance

messages.text: There has to be a setting for this somewhere.
messages.notes: Self-directed problem-solving that invites help

messages.text: Every time I log in I have to do this manually, there's got to be a better way.
messages.notes: Repetitive friction complaint, implying a feature or workflow request

messages.text: My colleague told me you can do X but I can't figure out how.
messages.notes: Social proof framing, implicit how-to request

messages.text: I just need this to work before my demo in two hours.
messages.notes: Urgency cue without a stated problem yet — context request implied

messages.text: I'm not very technical but something in my settings changed.
messages.notes: Self-deprecating opener + observation, implies need for guided help

messages.text: Hey there!
messages.notes: Pure greeting, no embedded request

messages.text: Good morning everyone.
messages.notes: Social opener to group, no addressee

messages.text: Is this the right place for support questions?
messages.notes: Channel orientation question, not yet a request

messages.text: Oh hey Bart, didn't know you were in here.
messages.notes: Acknowledging bot presence, no request yet

messages.text: Hi! First time using this channel.
messages.notes: New user introduction, no request embedded

messages.text: Hello, I have a question.
messages.notes: Pre-request opener, intent signal present but no content yet

messages.text: Is anyone around?
messages.notes: Presence check before committing to a request

messages.text: Bart, are you online?
messages.notes: Bot availability check, no request yet

messages.text: Hey quick question
messages.notes: Preamble to a question, no content yet

messages.text: Hi, I'm not sure who to ask about this.
messages.notes: Uncertainty about routing, invites redirection before request

messages.text: Anyone catch the game last night?
messages.notes: Purely social, off-topic for support bot

messages.text: Happy Friday everyone 🎉
messages.notes: Social filler, no actionable content

messages.text: Reminder: team standup at 2pm today.
messages.notes: Internal team coordination, not bot-directed

messages.text: Who's ordering lunch? I want in.
messages.notes: Purely social logistics, no relation to support

messages.text: PSA: there's cake in the break room.
messages.notes: Office social message, zero support relevance

messages.text: Can't believe it's already Q4.
messages.notes: Casual time observation, not actionable

messages.text: Does anyone have a good restaurant rec near the office?
messages.notes: Peer-to-peer social question, off-domain

messages.text: Mondays, am I right?
messages.notes: Social commiseration, zero actionable content

messages.text: Just pushed the latest release, heads up everyone.
messages.notes: Internal dev broadcast, may be relevant to team but not bot-directed

messages.text: Working from home today if anyone needs me.
messages.notes: Availability notice, peer-to-peer

messages.text: It's wild how much has changed in the last year.
messages.notes: Reflective statement, no clear intent or addressee

messages.text: I feel like things used to be simpler.
messages.notes: Vague sentiment, could precede a request but not yet one

messages.text: Bart, do you ever get tired of answering the same questions?
messages.notes: Bot-directed but not a support request — philosophical/social

messages.text: Sometimes I wonder if I'm doing this right.
messages.notes: Existential musing, could be work-related but no clear ask

messages.text: Honestly not sure what I expected.
messages.notes: Vague expression of disappointment or resignation, no specific target

messages.text: Bart, what do you actually know how to do?
messages.notes: Meta-question about bot capabilities, exploratory rather than task-oriented

messages.text: I keep going in circles with this.
messages.notes: Venting without a clear problem statement or addressee

messages.text: Why is everything always broken on Fridays.
messages.notes: Rhetorical frustration, could precede a support request but not yet one
